rules.name: Vague transformation claim
rules.description: H1 promises transformation but does not identify product type, target audience, mechanism, or output.

rules.name: Unlock headline
rules.description: H1 contains unlock, unleash, or semantic equivalents.

rules.name: Elevate headline
rules.description: H1 contains elevate your, take your ... to the next level, or similar.

rules.name: Reimagine headline
rules.description: H1 uses reimagine, reinvent, transform, or revolutionize without concrete detail.

rules.name: Future-oriented cliche
rules.description: H1 includes the future of, built for the future, or future-ready.

rules.name: Smarter promise
rules.description: H1 uses work smarter, build smarter, sell smarter, or similar generic comparative language.

rules.name: Not harder cliche
rules.description: H1 or subheadline contains not harder.

rules.name: Outcome without mechanism
rules.description: H1 states a desired result but gives no indication of how the product achieves it.

rules.name: Product noun missing
rules.description: H1 and first paragraph do not clearly state the product type (app, platform, service, marketplace, tool, extension, course).

rules.name: Audience missing
rules.description: H1 and subheadline do not identify who the product is for.

rules.name: Generic imperative structure
rules.description: H1 begins with a broad verb such as Create, Build, Grow, Scale, Transform, Discover, or Experience.

rules.name: Three-part slogan
rules.description: H1 uses three short benefits separated by commas, periods, or line breaks.

rules.name: Contrived contrast
rules.description: H1 follows Do X, not Y; More X, less Y; or From X to Y without product-specific meaning.

rules.name: All-in-one claim
rules.description: H1 or subheadline describes the product as an all-in-one platform, one platform, or everything in one place.

rules.name: One place cliche
rules.description: Copy contains everything you need in one place or semantic equivalent.

rules.name: Made simple cliche
rules.description: H1 ends with made simple, simplified, or without the complexity.

rules.name: Effortless promise
rules.description: H1 or subheadline claims the product is effortless without explaining the reduced work.

rules.name: Seamless promise
rules.description: H1 or subheadline uses seamless or seamlessly without describing the workflow.

rules.name: Generic emotional aspiration
rules.description: H1 promises confidence, freedom, growth, or possibility without tying it to a concrete task.

rules.name: Undefined superlative
rules.description: H1 uses best, ultimate, most powerful, leading, or world-class without evidence.

rules.name: Generic AI mention
rules.description: H1 uses AI-powered, powered by AI, or with AI without naming the AI capability.

rules.name: Magic framing
rules.description: H1 or subheadline uses magic, magically, in seconds, or at the click of a button.

rules.name: Excessively short slogan
rules.description: H1 is fewer than five words and cannot independently explain the value proposition.

rules.name: Excessively broad scope
rules.description: H1 could plausibly describe more than five unrelated product categories.

rules.name: Empty brand philosophy
rules.description: H1 communicates a brand attitude but not the product value.

rules.name: Generic better way claim
rules.description: Copy states a better way to... without explaining what is different.

rules.name: Next generation wording
rules.description: H1 includes next-gen, next generation, or equivalent hype.

rules.name: Game-changing wording
rules.description: Hero uses game-changing, change the game, or redefine.

rules.name: Abstract noun density
rules.description: Hero headline contains several abstract nouns but few concrete actions or objects.

rules.name: Headline interchangeable across industries
rules.description: Replacing the brand name would let the H1 fit SaaS, fintech, education, or ecommerce equally well.

rules.name: Generic supporting sentence
rules.description: Subheadline restates the headline without adding mechanism, audience, or proof.

rules.name: Vague benefits list
rules.description: Subheadline lists benefits such as faster, easier, smarter, or better without concrete detail.

rules.name: Three-adjective formula
rules.description: Sentence uses three positive adjectives, often in a list.

rules.name: Save time and money cliche
rules.description: Copy uses this phrase without quantification or context.

rules.name: Focus on what matters cliche
rules.description: Copy contains focus on what matters, focus on what you do best, or similar.

rules.name: From X to Y range
rules.description: Subheadline lists a broad range of use cases to imply universality.

rules.name: Whether you template
rules.description: Subheadline begins with or contains whether you are... followed by several audience types.

rules.name: Audience overbreadth
rules.description: More than three distinct audience types are targeted in one sentence.

rules.name: Benefit-only sentence
rules.description: Sentence contains outcomes but no product action or process.

rules.name: Mechanism-only vagueness
rules.description: Copy says the product uses AI, automation, or data without explaining what it does.

rules.name: Unverifiable speed claim
rules.description: Copy says in seconds, instantly, or faster than ever without baseline.

rules.name: Unverifiable simplicity claim
rules.description: Copy says no complexity, no hassle, without the headaches, or zero friction.

rules.name: Generic empowerment phrase
rules.description: empowers you to, helps you achieve, or enables teams to without specificity.

rules.name: Passive abstraction
rules.description: High use of nouns such as innovation, efficiency, productivity, growth, or transformation.

rules.name: No differentiated value
rules.description: Subheadline fails to say why the product is different from normal alternatives.

rules.name: No concrete object
rules.description: Subheadline contains no named deliverable, output, artifact, transaction, or user action.

rules.name: Empty trust wording
rules.description: Copy says trusted, secure, reliable, or proven without evidence.

rules.name: Universal applicability claim
rules.description: Product is described as suitable for any team, every business, or everyone.

rules.name: Feature pile-up
rules.description: Subheadline mentions four or more capabilities in one sentence.

rules.name: Excessive em-dash structure
rules.description: Sentence uses an em dash to append a generic benefit explanation.

rules.name: New badge
rules.description: Badge contains only New, Just launched, or Now available.

rules.name: Powered by AI badge
rules.description: Small pill states AI-powered without explaining functionality.

rules.name: Introducing badge
rules.description: Badge uses Introducing, Meet, or Say hello to.

rules.name: Version 2.0 badge
rules.description: Version badge is used as marketing copy without meaningful release information.

rules.name: Join thousands badge
rules.description: Eyebrow claims user volume without linked evidence.

rules.name: Built for badge
rules.description: Badge states a broad audience category without product specificity.

rules.name: Artificial urgency badge
rules.description: Limited access, Early access, Only X spots, or similar appears without support.

rules.name: Generic status badge
rules.description: Coming soon, Launching soon, or Beta appears while page presents mature social proof.

rules.name: Trend-label badge
rules.description: Badge contains Web3, AI-native, Next-gen, or another trend term as decoration.

rules.name: Badge repeats heading
rules.description: Eyebrow text adds no information beyond the section heading.

rules.name: Generic Get Started
rules.description: Primary CTA is exactly or semantically equivalent to Get Started.

rules.name: Generic Learn More
rules.description: CTA uses Learn More without identifying what will be learned.

rules.name: Generic Try Now
rules.description: CTA does not state the product action, cost, or next step.

rules.name: Generic Explore
rules.description: CTA is Explore, Discover, or See more with no object.

rules.name: Start for free without detail
rules.description: CTA does not explain trial duration, limitations, or signup requirements.

rules.name: Book a demo without context
rules.description: CTA exists but page does not identify who the demo is for.

rules.name: CTA destination ambiguity
rules.description: Label does not predict whether action opens signup, checkout, demo, waitlist, or contact form.

rules.name: CTA label inconsistency
rules.description: Same destination uses different labels across the page.

rules.name: Excessive CTA repetition
rules.description: Same CTA phrase appears three or more times.

rules.name: Multiple competing primary CTAs
rules.description: Two or more actions are worded as equally primary in the hero.

rules.name: Generic second CTA
rules.description: Secondary CTA uses See how it works, Watch demo, or Learn more without real content support.

rules.name: Artificially low-friction phrase
rules.description: Supporting copy says No credit card required, No commitment, or Cancel anytime without relevant pricing context.

rules.name: Imperative without value
rules.description: CTA is an action verb but does not communicate the immediate user benefit.

rules.name: Overpromising CTA
rules.description: Button uses Transform my business, Unlock growth, or another exaggerated outcome.

rules.name: Personal-pronoun manipulation
rules.description: CTA uses Start my journey, Grow my business, or similar emotional phrasing without product clarity.

rules.name: Urgency CTA
rules.description: Claim your spot, Get access now, or Don't miss out appears without real scarcity.

rules.name: Fake exclusivity
rules.description: CTA uses Get exclusive access when access is publicly available.

rules.name: Unclear download CTA
rules.description: Button says Download now without platform, file type, or system requirements.

rules.name: Generic app-store lead-in
rules.description: Copy says only Download the app without explaining what happens next.

rules.name: CTA-benefit mismatch
rules.description: CTA promises a result not directly delivered by the next step.

rules.name: Powerful features heading
rules.description: Section heading contains Powerful features, Everything you need, or equivalent.

rules.name: Built to scale feature
rules.description: Feature title uses this phrase without operational detail.

rules.name: Lightning fast feature
rules.description: Speed claim appears without measured latency or workflow comparison.

rules.name: Secure by design feature
rules.description: Security claim appears without named controls, standards, or practices.

rules.name: Easy to use feature
rules.description: Feature title or description claims ease without identifying reduced steps or learning burden.

rules.name: Seamless integration feature
rules.description: Integration claim lacks named services or setup steps.

rules.name: Smart automation feature
rules.description: Copy does not identify what is automated, when, or under whose control.

rules.name: Real-time insights feature
rules.description: No data source, update interval, decision, or example is named.

rules.name: Advanced analytics feature
rules.description: Analytics feature is described without metrics, reports, or outputs.

rules.name: Personalized experience feature
rules.description: Copy does not explain what changes and based on which signals.

rules.name: One-click claim
rules.description: One-click language is used without identifying prerequisites or actual workflow.

rules.name: Feature title as adjective pair
rules.description: Repeated titles such as Simple & Powerful, Fast & Secure, Flexible & Scalable.

rules.name: Equal-length feature descriptions
rules.description: Three or more sibling descriptions have unusually similar word count and sentence structure.

rules.name: Repeated grammatical template
rules.description: Features repeatedly follow Verb your noun with... or Get X with Y.

rules.name: Benefit duplication
rules.description: Multiple features make essentially the same speed, simplicity, or productivity claim.

rules.name: No concrete feature object
rules.description: Feature title contains no identifiable function, screen, operation, or output.

rules.name: Benefit mistaken for feature
rules.description: Titles such as Save time, Grow faster, or Stay focused appear as features.

rules.name: Technology mistaken for value
rules.description: Feature title is only AI-powered, Cloud-based, or Blockchain-enabled.

rules.name: Undefined smart adjective
rules.description: smart, intelligent, or adaptive appears without behavior definition.

rules.name: Undefined powerful adjective
rules.description: powerful appears more than once in feature copy.

rules.name: No limitations or conditions
rules.description: Features are described as universally successful and effortless.

rules.name: No user action
rules.description: Feature descriptions omit what the user actually does.

rules.name: No system action
rules.description: Feature descriptions omit what the product actually does.

rules.name: No output
rules.description: Feature descriptions omit what is generated, saved, changed, or delivered.

rules.name: Multi-feature sentence
rules.description: One feature description includes three or more separate capabilities.

rules.name: Generic collaboration claim
rules.description: Collaborate effortlessly, Keep everyone aligned, or similar without mechanics.

rules.name: Generic workflow claim
rules.description: Streamline your workflow without naming a workflow step.

rules.name: Generic productivity claim
rules.description: Boost productivity without baseline, process, or example.

rules.name: Generic growth claim
rules.description: Drive growth, Accelerate growth, or Scale faster without mechanism.

rules.name: Features interchangeable across products
rules.description: Feature copy could be reused for several unrelated SaaS products.

rules.name: Generic three-step flow
rules.description: Process consists of Sign up then Customize then Get results or semantic equivalent.

rules.name: Registration as product step
rules.description: Create an account is counted as a core product step.

rules.name: Vague setup step
rules.description: Step uses Set up, Customize, or Configure without specifying inputs.

rules.name: Vague result step
rules.description: Final step says See results, Grow, Enjoy, or Succeed.

rules.name: No meaningful system process
rules.description: Steps describe user actions but not product processing.

rules.name: No user input detail
rules.description: Process does not specify what data, file, URL, choice, or asset the user provides.

rules.name: No output detail
rules.description: Process does not identify the concrete result returned.

rules.name: Unrealistically few steps
rules.description: Complex service is presented as three effortless actions with no conditions.

rules.name: Equal-length process descriptions
rules.description: Step descriptions follow identical sentence patterns.

rules.name: Magic happens step
rules.description: Copy uses magic or invisible automation instead of explaining the process.

rules.name: Generic automation step
rules.description: Let AI do the rest or semantic equivalent.

rules.name: Missing failure path
rules.description: Process implies guaranteed success with no review, correction, or user control.

rules.name: No time expectation
rules.description: Time-sensitive process gives no realistic completion estimate.

rules.name: No prerequisites
rules.description: Process hides required account, integration, payment, data, or permissions.

rules.name: Process duplicates feature copy
rules.description: How it works merely repeats benefits instead of explaining steps.

rules.name: Trusted by thousands
rules.description: Trust claim lacks exact number, source, or verification.

rules.name: Loved by teams worldwide
rules.description: Broad affection claim without evidence.

rules.name: Unnamed user count
rules.description: Thousands of users, teams everywhere, or equivalent.

rules.name: Round-number metric
rules.description: Metrics use values such as 10K+, 50K+, 100+, or 99% without source.

rules.name: Unsupported growth metric
rules.description: Growth, retention, conversion, or savings statistic lacks methodology.

rules.name: Unsupported performance multiplier
rules.description: Claims such as 10x faster, 3x more productive, or 5x growth.

rules.name: Unsupported customer logos
rules.description: Copy says Trusted by above unlinked logos without case studies.

rules.name: Premature social proof
rules.description: Coming-soon or early-stage product displays large adoption claims.

rules.name: Generic award claim
rules.description: Award-winning, industry-leading, or recognized without named award.

rules.name: Generic press claim
rules.description: As seen in without linked coverage.

rules.name: Perfect rating claim
rules.description: 5.0 rating or five stars without platform or review count.

rules.name: No metric labels
rules.description: Large figures appear without timeframe, unit, or definition.

rules.name: Mixed metric granularity
rules.description: Precise percentages are shown beside vague rounded counts.

rules.name: Vanity metrics only
rules.description: All social proof measures users, downloads, or hours saved but not outcomes.

rules.name: No evidence destination
rules.description: Social-proof element contains no link to customer, review, report, or methodology.

rules.name: Generic praise only
rules.description: Quote could apply to any software product.

rules.name: No concrete before state
rules.description: Testimonial does not describe the previous problem.

rules.name: No concrete product action
rules.description: Quote does not identify what feature or workflow was used.

rules.name: No measurable outcome
rules.description: Quote does not contain a specific result, improvement, or changed behavior.

rules.name: Identical testimonial length
rules.description: Three or more testimonials have very similar word count.

rules.name: Identical testimonial structure
rules.description: Quotes follow We struggled with X. Product changed everything. Now Y.

rules.name: Excessive enthusiasm
rules.description: Multiple quotes contain superlatives, exclamation marks, or emotional exaggeration.

rules.name: Game changer phrase
rules.description: Quote uses game changer, changed everything, or equivalent.

rules.name: Can't imagine life without it
rules.description: Generic dependency statement appears.

rules.name: Saved us hours without number
rules.description: Time-saving claim lacks baseline or period.

rules.name: Generic role titles
rules.description: Roles such as Founder, CEO, Designer, Marketer appear without organization context.

rules.name: Suspiciously balanced identities
rules.description: Testimonials appear constructed to cover multiple ideal customer types evenly.

rules.name: Generic company names
rules.description: Names such as Acme, Nova, Horizon, Bright Labs, or similar appear.

rules.name: First-name-only identity
rules.description: Quote provides insufficient identifying information.

rules.name: No verification link
rules.description: Customer identity cannot be verified.

rules.name: Same voice across customers
rules.description: Vocabulary, punctuation and sentence rhythm are unusually consistent.

rules.name: Marketing vocabulary in customer voice
rules.description: Customers use the same phrases as the landing-page copy.

rules.name: Product name repeated unnaturally
rules.description: Each quote explicitly names the product in a promotional manner.

rules.name: No criticism or nuance
rules.description: Every quote is uniformly perfect and unconditional.

rules.name: Testimonial as feature summary
rules.description: Quote appears written to communicate a marketing feature rather than real experience.

rules.name: Generic plan names
rules.description: Plans use Free, Starter, Pro, Business, or Enterprise without audience clarity.

rules.name: For individuals vagueness
rules.description: Plan description targets a generic user type without usage scenario.

rules.name: For growing teams cliche
rules.description: Plan targets growth stage rather than concrete needs.

rules.name: Everything in wording
rules.description: Higher plan says Everything in Pro, plus... repeatedly.

rules.name: Undefined credits
rules.description: Pricing uses credits, tokens, units, runs, or generations without explanation.

rules.name: Undefined usage limits
rules.description: Limits are presented without examples of normal consumption.

rules.name: Vague unlimited claim
rules.description: Unlimited appears with hidden or unclear restrictions.

rules.name: Most popular without basis
rules.description: Recommended plan label has no explanation.

rules.name: Best value claim
rules.description: Plan is labelled best value without transparent comparison.

rules.name: Missing billing context
rules.description: Monthly price does not clarify annual commitment or billing frequency.

rules.name: Missing tax information
rules.description: Price copy does not clarify whether tax is included where relevant.

rules.name: Missing renewal information
rules.description: Trial or promotional pricing lacks renewal terms.

rules.name: Missing cancellation information
rules.description: Subscription copy lacks cancellation details.

rules.name: Missing overage information
rules.description: Usage-limited plan does not explain what happens after the limit.

rules.name: Feature-limit mixing
rules.description: Feature names and consumption limits are mixed into one unclear list.

rules.name: Artificial feature padding
rules.description: Plans include generic support or analytics items mainly to lengthen lists.

rules.name: Enterprise vagueness
rules.description: Enterprise plan says custom, flexible, secure, or tailored without specifics.

rules.name: Contact-sales boilerplate
rules.description: Enterprise description is interchangeable with any SaaS company.

rules.name: Unsupported savings percentage
rules.description: Annual toggle advertises savings without transparent calculation.

rules.name: Pricing-value mismatch
rules.description: Copy does not connect price to concrete usage or outcomes.

rules.name: Generic What is question
rules.description: FAQ simply repeats the product description.

rules.name: Generic How does it work
rules.description: Answer repeats marketing copy instead of workflow.

rules.name: Generic Is it easy to use
rules.description: Question appears designed to repeat a simplicity claim.

rules.name: Generic Is it secure
rules.description: Answer provides reassurance but no security details.

rules.name: Generic Can I cancel anytime
rules.description: Answer lacks billing-cycle or refund specifics.

rules.name: Generic Who is it for
rules.description: Answer lists a very broad audience.

rules.name: Generic Do I need experience
rules.description: Answer uses No experience required without discussing actual learning needs.

rules.name: FAQ repeats pricing cards
rules.description: Questions add no detail beyond visible plan copy.

rules.name: FAQ hides essential terms
rules.description: Important pricing, privacy or limitation details appear only in FAQ.

rules.name: Overly polished answer symmetry
rules.description: Answers have similar length and structure across questions.

rules.name: No difficult questions
rules.description: FAQ contains only convenient questions that reinforce benefits.

rules.name: No limitation questions
rules.description: No answer addresses failure, eligibility, support, compatibility, or constraints.

rules.name: Vague integration answer
rules.description: We integrate with your favorite tools without naming them.

rules.name: Vague support answer
rules.description: Our team is always here to help without hours, channel, or response time.

rules.name: Vague refund answer
rules.description: Uses contact us without policy detail.

rules.name: AI capability overstatement
rules.description: FAQ implies AI always produces correct or high-quality results.

rules.name: Privacy reassurance without policy
rules.description: Answer claims data safety without linking to privacy terms.

rules.name: Placeholder future answer
rules.description: More features are coming soon substitutes for current capability detail.

rules.name: Duplicate wording
rules.description: FAQ repeats exact phrases from hero and feature sections.

rules.name: Question-set interchangeability
rules.description: FAQ set could be reused for unrelated products.

rules.name: Ready to formula
rules.description: Final section begins with Ready to transform/grow/build/start...

rules.name: Start your journey cliche
rules.description: Final CTA uses journey language.

rules.name: Join thousands repetition
rules.description: Final CTA repeats unsupported social proof.

rules.name: Empty urgency
rules.description: Copy says Start today, Don't wait, or Now is the time.

rules.name: Hero repetition
rules.description: Final CTA repeats the same headline promise without new proof.

rules.name: No risk clarification
rules.description: Final CTA pushes signup without mentioning cost, trial, requirement, or next step.

rules.name: Emotional overreach
rules.description: Final CTA promises transformation disproportionate to the immediate action.

rules.name: Generic confidence close
rules.description: Copy uses See the difference, Experience the future, or Take control.

rules.name: Fake scarcity close
rules.description: Final CTA uses limited-access wording without real availability limits.

rules.name: No concrete action outcome
rules.description: User cannot infer what happens after clicking.

rules.name: Uniform neutral optimism
rules.description: Nearly all text uses safe, positive, frictionless language.

rules.name: No distinctive vocabulary
rules.description: Copy contains no recurring product-specific words, metaphors, or verbal devices.

rules.name: Tone interchangeability
rules.description: Tone could belong to hundreds of modern SaaS products.

rules.name: Inconsistent formality
rules.description: Page switches between casual and formal language.

rules.name: Inconsistent person
rules.description: Copy switches between you, your team, users, and third person without reason.

rules.name: Inconsistent imperative style
rules.description: Some CTAs use verbs, others nouns, others emotional promises.

rules.name: Brand name overuse
rules.description: Product name is repeated unnaturally in several sections.

rules.name: Brand name underuse
rules.description: Product name and category are absent after the hero.

rules.name: No point of view
rules.description: Copy avoids any opinion, tradeoff, limitation, or strong position.

rules.name: No audience language
rules.description: Copy does not use terminology familiar to its intended users.

rules.name: Corporate-vague tone
rules.description: Uses solutions, capabilities, ecosystem, innovation, or excellence excessively.

rules.name: Startup-hype tone
rules.description: Uses disrupt, game-changing, redefine, next-gen, or revolutionary.

rules.name: AI-helper tone
rules.description: Frequent phrases such as help you, empower you, and enable you.

rules.name: No negative reality
rules.description: Copy avoids discussing difficulty, risk, cost, or user effort.

rules.name: Excessive reassurance
rules.description: Frequent use of simple, easy, secure, reliable, and hassle-free.

rules.name: Emotional sameness
rules.description: Every section has the same motivational intensity.

rules.name: Heading-template repetition
rules.description: Several headings follow the same grammatical construction.

rules.name: Repeated rhetorical contrast
rules.description: Multiple sections use not just X, but Y or equivalent.

rules.name: Repeated triads
rules.description: Copy repeatedly groups ideas in sets of three.

rules.name: No memorable sentence
rules.description: No sentence contains a distinctive, product-ownable formulation.

rules.name: No product category
rules.description: First 150 words do not identify what the product is.

rules.name: No target audience
rules.description: First 150 words do not identify who it is for.

rules.name: No user input
rules.description: Page never explains what users provide to the product.

rules.name: No system action
rules.description: Page never explains what the product does with the input.

rules.name: No concrete output
rules.description: Page never identifies what users receive.

rules.name: No usage context
rules.description: Page never describes when or why someone would use the product.

rules.name: No domain terms
rules.description: Copy has unusually low density of industry-specific nouns.

rules.name: No examples
rules.description: Page contains no concrete use case, example, or scenario.

rules.name: No numbers
rules.description: Page contains no meaningful limits, timeframes, prices, quantities, or performance data.

rules.name: Numbers without definitions
rules.description: Metrics appear without units, baselines, or timeframe.

rules.name: No named integrations
rules.description: Integration copy exists but no specific platform is named.

rules.name: No named deliverables
rules.description: Outputs are called results, insights, content, or solutions without exact form.

rules.name: No comparison
rules.description: Page does not contrast product workflow with the current alternative.

rules.name: No limitation
rules.description: Page does not state what the product cannot or does not do.

rules.name: No prerequisites
rules.description: Page does not mention required account, data, device, skill, or setup.

rules.name: No timing detail
rules.description: Claims speed but gives no actual expected duration.

rules.name: No pricing detail
rules.description: Pricing language exists but essential billing behavior is omitted.

rules.name: No geographic context
rules.description: Local service does not name supported cities, countries, or availability.

rules.name: No eligibility context
rules.description: Product with access conditions does not explain eligibility.

rules.name: High abstract-to-concrete ratio
rules.description: Abstract nouns significantly outnumber concrete domain nouns.

rules.name: Repeated sentence openings
rules.description: Three or more paragraphs begin with the same word or phrase.

rules.name: Repeated With construction
rules.description: Multiple descriptions begin with With [product/feature]...

rules.name: Repeated Whether construction
rules.description: Two or more sections use Whether you...

rules.name: Repeated From-to construction
rules.description: Appears in multiple sections.

rules.name: Repeated Designed to construction
rules.description: Appears in several feature descriptions.

rules.name: Repeated Built for construction
rules.description: Appears in hero, features, pricing, or footer.

rules.name: Repeated So you can ending
rules.description: Several descriptions end with the same benefit clause.

rules.name: Repeated three-item lists
rules.description: Page repeatedly uses exactly three comma-separated benefits.

rules.name: Similar paragraph lengths
rules.description: Most feature descriptions fall within a narrow word-count range.

rules.name: Similar sentence counts
rules.description: Every card uses exactly one or two sentences.

rules.name: Identical punctuation rhythm
rules.description: Sibling cards share the same punctuation structure.

rules.name: Repeated em dash
rules.description: Multiple section descriptions use identical claim-explanation form.

rules.name: Repeated colon headings
rules.description: Several headings use Feature: Benefit construction.

rules.name: Vocabulary recycling
rules.description: Same promotional terms repeat across many sections.

rules.name: Benefit recycling
rules.description: Speed, ease, growth, and productivity claims recur without new detail.

rules.name: CTA recycling
rules.description: Generic CTA phrases repeat in every major section.

rules.name: Repeated section ending
rules.description: Multiple sections close with a positive future-oriented sentence.

rules.name: Section-template similarity
rules.description: Text structure across sections is highly parallel.

rules.name: Testimonial-copy similarity
rules.description: Quotes have high semantic or stylistic similarity.

rules.name: FAQ-answer similarity
rules.description: Answers repeat the same reassurance formula.

rules.name: Promotional cliche density
rules.description: Multiple landing-page cliche words appear with low concrete-information density.

rules.name: Percentage improvement without support
rules.description: Percentage improvement lacks baseline, sample, timeframe, or source.

rules.name: Speed multiplier without support
rules.description: Speed multiplier lacks compared process, start/end conditions, or methodology.

rules.name: User count without support
rules.description: User count lacks exact count or range, date, or verification source.

rules.name: Time saved without support
rules.description: Time saved lacks user group, workflow, or measured period.

rules.name: Cost saved without support
rules.description: Cost saved lacks original cost, new cost, or assumptions.

rules.name: Security claim without support
rules.description: Security claim lacks named controls, standard, certification, or policy.

rules.name: Compliance claim without support
rules.description: Compliance claim lacks named framework, jurisdiction, or status.

rules.name: Customer trust claim without support
rules.description: Customer trust claim lacks linked customer, review platform, or case study.

rules.name: Market leadership without support
rules.description: Market leadership lacks named category, source, report, or award.

rules.name: AI accuracy without support
rules.description: AI accuracy claim lacks dataset, benchmark, metric, or limitation.

rules.name: Sustainability claim without support
rules.description: Sustainability claim lacks calculation method, scope, or data source.

rules.name: Availability claim without support
rules.description: Availability claim lacks supported geography, device, plan, or rollout status.

rules.name: Unlimited claim without support
rules.description: Unlimited claim lacks fair-use limits, exclusions, or throttling rules.

rules.name: Guaranteed outcome without support
rules.description: Guaranteed outcome lacks eligibility, conditions, or refund/guarantee terms.

rules.name: Real-time claim without support
rules.description: Real-time claim lacks actual update interval and data source.

rules.name: Coming soon + large user base
rules.description: Page states not launched while claiming thousands of users.

rules.name: Free + payment requirement
rules.description: Hero says free but pricing or signup requires payment.

rules.name: No credit card + card requirement
rules.description: Copy contradicts checkout flow or pricing detail.

rules.name: Unlimited + listed limit
rules.description: Same feature is described as unlimited and capped.

rules.name: Instant + delayed delivery
rules.description: Hero claims instant output while FAQ states manual review or waiting period.

rules.name: Global + limited geography
rules.description: Copy says worldwide but availability is local.

rules.name: No setup + onboarding requirements
rules.description: Product requires integrations, configuration, or data upload.

rules.name: Fully automated + human service
rules.description: Page claims automation but process relies on manual support.

rules.name: Secure + missing policy
rules.description: Strong security claim with no privacy or security documentation.

rules.name: All-in-one + external dependencies
rules.description: Product requires many external tools for core functionality.

rules.name: Beginner-friendly + technical prerequisites
rules.description: Copy hides required expertise.

rules.name: Cancel anytime + annual lock-in
rules.description: Pricing terms contradict cancellation language.

rules.name: One-click + multi-step process
rules.description: Marketing copy contradicts actual workflow.

rules.name: Personalization + no user data
rules.description: Product claims personalization but collects no relevant signals.

rules.name: Real-time + static data
rules.description: Product representation or documentation indicates batch updates.
